title: artemisia commutata var. douglasiana
intro: Artemisia commutata var. douglasiana, also known as Douglas's sagebrush, is a subspecies of the sagebrush plant native to North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of camphor and thujone, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Douglas's sagebrush include relief from colds, fever, and sore throats, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Douglas's sagebrush can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves for topical application, making it a versatile and useful addition to traditional herbal medicine.
